skill.name: delon-cache-caching-strategies
skill.description: Implement @delon/cache patterns for Angular applications, including memory cache, LocalStorage, SessionStorage, TTL expiration, invalidation, namespaced keys, and HTTP response caching.
skill.summary: Add @delon/cache strategies for Angular data, preferences, and HTTP responses.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals, not executable Ruby or shell code. The skill has no prompt-injection text and no confirmed malicious behavior, but it teaches persistent browser storage and broad HTTP response caching that require careful scoping to avoid retaining sensitive data.

medium_findings.title: Broad HTTP Response Caching Example
medium_findings.description: The interceptor example caches all GET responses unless a skip header or realtime URL is present. This is useful for performance, but copied directly it could cache user-specific or sensitive API responses without endpoint allowlists or Cache-Control checks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly caches GET HttpResponse objects by URL and only excludes a narrow set of requests. The risk depends on how users adapt the example, so this is a medium-confidence implementation guidance issue rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Browser Storage Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends LocalStorage and SessionStorage cache modes for preferences, configuration, and general TTL guidance. Browser storage is legitimate for this caching skill, but persistent storage should not be used for tokens, secrets, regulated data, or sensitive user records.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced examples explicitly select localStorage and sessionStorage. The skill does not ask users to store secrets, but the guidance needs a security warning because browser storage is often misused for sensitive data.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick detections point to Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals inside documentation examples. No evidence found of executable shell commands, Ruby backticks, process spawning, or command injection logic in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows code fences and TypeScript interpolation, not shell execution. There are no runtime scripts or files besides SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Reference Links Are Benign External URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are documentation references for @delon/cache, AWS caching guidance, and Azure cache-aside patterns. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs or instructs automated network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a References section. They are not used in executable code or data-transfer instructions.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Identifier False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections map to ordinary words and route fragments such as method, realtime, descriptive cache keys, and checklist text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, encryption, hashing, or cryptographic implementation guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited line found no cryptographic algorithm usage. The matches are unrelated text in caching documentation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Cache API Examples
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections point to cache methods such as has, remove, clear, Set, and key registration. These are ordinary @delon/cache usage examples and do not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code only manipulates application cache keys and values. No host enumeration, process inspection, filesystem traversal, or environment discovery is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Cache Key Includes Full Request URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The HTTP cache example derives cache keys from the full URL and parameters. This can persist sensitive query parameters in cache keys or logs if applications place private data in URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly uses req.urlWithParams and logs the cache key. The sensitivity depends on application URL design, so the finding is a contextual warning.

content.value_statement: Angular apps often repeat API calls and lose cache behavior across views. This skill provides @delon/cache patterns for memory, session, local storage, TTLs, invalidation, and HTTP caching.
actual_capabilities: Configures @delon/cache providers with default TTL and request settings.
Shows wrapper services for memory, LocalStorage, and SessionStorage cache modes.
Implements get-or-set caching for Observable and Promise data sources.
Demonstrates manual invalidation and prefix-based cache grouping patterns.
Adds an Angular HTTP interceptor example for caching GET responses.
Explains cache key naming, TTL choices, and implementation checklists.
limitations: Examples are documentation snippets and may need project-specific imports and types.
The skill does not validate whether cached data is safe for browser storage.
The HTTP interceptor example needs endpoint allowlists for sensitive applications.
It does not provide automated tests or runtime cache monitoring code.

use_cases.title: Reduce repeated API calls
use_cases.description: Add memory caching and TTL rules for task lists, configuration data, and repeated read requests.

use_cases.title: Persist user interface preferences
use_cases.description: Store theme, language, and layout preferences with LocalStorage or SessionStorage cache modes.

use_cases.title: Standardize cache invalidation
use_cases.description: Create naming, grouping, and invalidation patterns that teams can reuse across Angular services.

prompt_templates.title: Add a basic cache service
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add an @delon/cache wrapper service for my Angular app. Include memory cache defaults, get, set, remove, and clear methods.

prompt_templates.title: Cache preferences safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to cache non-sensitive user preferences with LocalStorage. Include TTL settings and a clear method for account logout.

prompt_templates.title: Design invalidation rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design cache keys and invalidation rules for task create, update, delete, and list views in an Angular service.

prompt_templates.title: Review HTTP caching strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Angular HTTP cache interceptor. Recommend endpoint allowlists, TTL values, skip conditions, and sensitive-data safeguards.

output_examples.input: Request: Add caching for a task list that reloads often.
output_examples.output: The response outlines a cache key, a five-minute memory TTL, a cache-first load flow, and invalidation after task changes.

output_examples.input: Request: Persist theme and language settings.
output_examples.output: The response recommends LocalStorage for non-sensitive preferences, a 30-day TTL, typed preference fields, and a clear operation.
The response proposes an interceptor with explicit skip rules, short TTLs, endpoint allowlists, and warnings for private data.

best_practices: Cache only data that has a clear owner, TTL, and invalidation path.
Use LocalStorage and SessionStorage only for non-sensitive browser data.
Prefer explicit endpoint allowlists before caching HTTP responses.
anti_patterns: Do not cache authentication tokens, secrets, or regulated records in browser storage.
Do not cache every GET request without checking endpoint sensitivity and cache headers.
Do not use generic cache keys that collide across users, tenants, or feature areas.

faq.question: Does this skill install @delon/cache?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples. Your project must already support the needed Angular and @delon/cache packages.

faq.question: Can it use memory cache only?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include memory cache as the default mode for short-lived application data.

faq.question: Is LocalStorage safe for all cached data?
faq.answer: No. Use LocalStorage only for non-sensitive data such as preferences or public configuration.

faq.question: Does the HTTP interceptor cache POST requests?
faq.answer: No. The documented interceptor caches GET responses and passes other methods through.

faq.question: How does the skill handle cache invalidation?
faq.answer: It shows manual removal, grouped key tracking, and cache updates after create or update operations.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
